content.problemStatement:
You are given two binary strings AAA and BBB, each of length NNN.

Consider a non-empty substring A′A'A′ of AAA and a non-empty substring B′B'B′ of BBB, both of the same length. Let X=A′⊕B′X = A' \oplus B'X=A′⊕B′ be the string obtained by taking the exclusive OR of A′A'A′ and B′B'B′.

You are also given a function f(X)f(X)f(X), defined as
f(X)=⌊∣X∣2X10⌋f(X) = \left \lfloor \dfrac{|X|}{2^{X_{10}}} \right \rfloorf(X)=⌊2X10​∣X∣​⌋

where ∣X∣|X|∣X∣ denotes the length of XXX and X10X_{10}X10​ denotes the decimal value of the binary integer represented by XXX. For example, if X=0110101X = 0110101X=0110101, then ∣X∣=7|X| = 7∣X∣=7 and X10=53X_{10} = 53X10​=53, so f(X)=⌊7/253⌋=0f(X) = \lfloor 7 / 2^{53} \rfloor = 0f(X)=⌊7/253⌋=0.

Your task is to **maximize** the value of the function f(X)f(X)f(X) for the given strings AAA and BBB, by choosing A′A'A′ and B′B'B′ appropriately.

**Note:** The exclusive OR of two binary strings AAA and BBB such that ∣A∣=∣B∣=k|A| = |B| = k∣A∣=∣B∣=k is the unique binary string XXX of length kkk such that Xi=1X_i = 1Xi​=1 **if and only if** Ai≠BiA_i \neq B_iAi​=Bi​.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT — the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
Each test case consists of three lines of input.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN — the length of the binary strings AAA and BBB.
The second line of each test case contains a binary string AAA of length NNN.
The third line of each test case contains a binary string BBB of length NNN.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the maximum value of the function.
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases does not exceed 5*10^5
A and B consist only of characters '0' and '1'
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case: first line contains integer N, the length of the binary strings. The second line contains a binary string A of length N. The third line contains a binary string B of length N.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the maximum possible value of f(X).

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: We calculate the value of f(X)f(X)f(X) for a few choices of XXX:

If A′=10A' = 10A′=10 and B′=01B' = 01B′=01, then X=11X = 11X=11. This gives f(X)=⌊2/23⌋=⌊2/8⌋=0f(X) = \lfloor 2 / 2^3 \rfloor = \lfloor 2 / 8 \rfloor = 0f(X)=⌊2/23⌋=⌊2/8⌋=0
If A′=01A' = 01A′=01 and B′=10B' = 10B′=10, then X=11X = 11X=11. This gives f(X)=⌊2/23⌋=0f(X) = \lfloor 2 / 2^3 \rfloor = 0f(X)=⌊2/23⌋=0
If A′=10A' = 10A′=10 and B′=10B' = 10B′=10, then X=00X = 00X=00. This gives f(X)=⌊2/20⌋=2f(X) = \lfloor 2 / 2^0 \rfloor = 2f(X)=⌊2/20⌋=2
If A′=101A' = 101A′=101 and B′=010B' = 010B′=010, then X=111X = 111X=111. This gives f(X)=⌊3/27⌋=0f(X) = \lfloor 3 / 2^7 \rfloor = 0f(X)=⌊3/27⌋=0

It can be verified that the maximum value of fff is 222.

solution.approach: The function f(X) = floor(|X| / 2^{X10}) is maximized when the denominator is as small as possible. The denominator is 2^{X10}, where X10 is the decimal value of the XOR string X. The smallest possible value of X10 is 0, which happens exactly when X consists only of '0's, i.e., when the two chosen substrings are identical. In that case f(X) = |X|. Therefore the problem reduces to finding the longest common substring (contiguous) between A and B. The answer is the length of that substring; if no common character exists the answer is 0. To compute the longest common substring efficiently we build a suffix automaton for string A in O(N) time and then walk through string B, maintaining the length of the current match. The maximum length encountered is the required answer.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case (building the automaton and scanning B). Overall O(total N) ≤ 5·10^5.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) for the automaton (at most 2*N states).

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().split()
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it))
        A = next(it).strip()
        B = next(it).strip()
        # suffix automaton for A
        nexts = []
        link = []
        length = []
        nexts.append([-1, -1])
        link.append(-1)
        length.append(0)
        last = 0
        for ch in A:
            c = ord(ch) - 48
            cur = len(nexts)
            nexts.append([-1, -1])
            length.append(length[last] + 1)
            link.append(0)
            p = last
            while p != -1 and nexts[p][c] == -1:
                nexts[p][c] = cur
                p = link[p]
            if p == -1:
                link[cur] = 0
            else:
                q = nexts[p][c]
                if length[p] + 1 == length[q]:
                    link[cur] = q
                else:
                    clone = len(nexts)
                    nexts.append(nexts[q][:])
                    length.append(length[p] + 1)
                    link.append(link[q])
                    while p != -1 and nexts[p][c] == q:
                        nexts[p][c] = clone
                        p = link[p]
                    link[q] = link[cur] = clone
            last = cur
        v = 0
        l = 0
        ans = 0
        for ch in B:
            c = ord(ch) - 48
            while v and nexts[v][c] == -1:
                v = link[v]
                l = length[v]
            if nexts[v][c] != -1:
                v = nexts[v][c]
                l += 1
            else:
                v = 0
                l = 0
            if l > ans:
                ans = l
        out.append(str(ans))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
